config.empty_state_message: Record the item's barcode identifier.
Record the primary (common) name for this item.
Record alternative names for this item.
Record the material or substance composition of this item (e.g., pigment, mineral, organic compound).
Record the origination date of this item (EDTF).
Record the date this item was acquired (EDTF).
Record the geographic origin of this item.
Record the manufacturer of this item.
Record where this item is currently stored or located (e.g., storage room, cabinet, shelf, or external repository).
Describe the current storage location, such as a grid location.
Record chemical analysis data — including chemical material types, identifiers, and supporting data assignments.
Record the chemical name of the material.
Record elements detected through chemical or spectrometric analysis of this item, along with the analysis event and supporting references.
Record GHS (Globally Harmonized System) and NFPA hazard classifications applicable to this item.
Add free-text hazard descriptions, handling precautions, or other safety notes for this item.
Link Safety Data Sheets (SDS), MSDS files, or other official safety documents related to this item.
If this item is a mixture or composite material, record the type of mixture (e.g., solution, suspension, alloy).
Record the individual components that make up this mixture, including their materials and proportional dimensions.
This resource has no names yet. Add at least a primary name so it can be located in search and citations.
Record the item category to classify this resource (e.g., sample, reference standard, archive material).

config.custom_card_name: Type of Reference or Sample Collection Item
config.empty_state_message: Assign a type to clarify what kind of reference or sample collection item this represents.
If this item is a reference standard (e.g., NIST, museum reference), record the standard type here.
Record the attribute type that further qualifies the classification of this item.
Descriptive statements or interpretive notes about this resource (e.g., physical description, condition, remarks) appear here.
Add accession numbers, catalog IDs, lot numbers, or other system identifiers for this item.
Record the material or substance composition of this item (e.g., pigment, mineral, organic compound).
Record physical measurements for this item — weight, volume, quantity, or other numeric dimensions with units.
Record the color or colorimetric measurements of this item, including any color space values and additional notes.

config.custom_card_name: Parent Reference or Sample Collection Item
config.empty_state_message: If this item is a sub-sample or portion derived from a larger specimen, link the parent reference or sample collection item here.
Link the collection or set that this item belongs to.
Record the institution or individual who currently holds legal ownership of this item.
Document how and when this item was acquired — including acquisition type, transferring parties, date, and source references.
Record the date this item was acquired (EDTF).
Record where this item is currently stored or located (e.g., storage room, cabinet, shelf, or external repository).
Describe the current storage location, such as a grid location.
If this is a sample removed from a larger object, record the sampling event — including the source object, technique, carried-out-by, and date.
Record when and by whom this item was made, synthesized, collected, or otherwise produced — including timespan, location, and causative factors.
Record the origination date of this item (EDTF).
Link to images, documents, data files, or other digital resources associated with this item.
